mission.title: kubeflow: API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a large number of profiles
mission.description: API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a large number of profiles. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
[A clear and concise description of what the bug is.]

We started seeing some slow startup time when a user loads the Kubeflow dashboard, and after some tracing tracked down a slow call to

steps.title: Apply the fix for API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a large
steps.description:
To solve https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/issues/5190

Found most of the issue to occur when listing the rolebindings when there are many namespaces as it does a loop. Query the shared index to list and get the rolebindings.

* Note: first commit and if there are procedures I am missing, please
```yaml
GET /kfam/v1/bindings?user=$USER ReadBinding 6.064392368s
```

steps.title: Confirm API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
To solve https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/issues/5190

Found most of the issue to occur when listing the rolebindings when there are many namespaces as it does a loop. Query the shared index to list and get the rolebindings.

* Note: first commit and if there are procedures I am missing, please let me know.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
